name: Amish Friendship Bread - Sourdough
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix all the starter ingredients well and place in a medium-sized glass bowl. Cover with a dinner plate so as not to cover tightly. Let stand overnight in a warm place. Stir down each day for 4 days (Important - do not refrigerate batter at any time).
2. On the fifth day, add flour, milk, and sugar and stir. Stir down each day until the 10th day.
3. On the 10th day, add flour, milk, and sugar and measure out 3 separate cups of starter. Give one cup starter and a copy of the instructions to each of three friends.
4. Use the remaining dough to bake your choice of bread or use in a streusel recipe as follows:
Friendship Starter Streusel:
Batter:
- 2/3 cup cooking oil
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking soda
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 3 eggs
Streusel:
- 1/3 cup margarine, melted
- 1/2 cup white sugar
- 1/2 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 cup quick oats
- 1 cup nuts (optional)
5. Mix oil, sugar, flour, baking soda, salt, and eggs with the remaining starter. Mix well and spoon half of the batter into a greased and floured 9 x 13 baking pan.
6. To prepare streusel, combine all ingredients and mix well. Sprinkle half of the streusel over the batter. Cover with the remaining batter and streusel.
7. Bake at 350 degrees for 35-40 minutes.
